name: newspaper3k
documentation:
# Newspaper3k: Article scraping & curation

```{image} https://badge.fury.io/py/newspaper3k.svg
:target: |-
:  http://badge.fury.io/py/newspaper3k.svg
:  :alt: Latest version
```

```{image} https://travis-ci.org/codelucas/newspaper.svg
:alt: Build status
:target: http://travis-ci.org/codelucas/newspaper/
```

```{image} https://coveralls.io/repos/github/codelucas/newspaper/badge.svg?branch=master
:alt: Coverage status
:target: https://coveralls.io/github/codelucas/newspaper
```

Inspired by [requests] for its simplicity and powered by [lxml] for its speed:

> "Newspaper is an amazing python library for extracting & curating articles."
> -- [tweeted by] Kenneth Reitz, Author of [requests]
>
> "Newspaper delivers Instapaper style article extraction." -- [The Changelog]

**Newspaper is a Python3 library**! Or, view our **deprecated and buggy** [Python2 branch]

## A Glance:

```pycon
>>> from newspaper import Article

>>> url = 'http://fox13now.com/2013/12/30/new-year-new-laws-obamacare-pot-guns-and-drones/'
>>> article = Article(url)
```

```pycon
>>> article.download()

>>> article.html
'<!DOCTYPE HTML><html itemscope itemtype="http://...'
```

```pycon
>>> article.parse()

>>> article.authors
['Leigh Ann Caldwell', 'John Honway']

>>> article.publish_date
datetime.datetime(2013, 12, 30, 0, 0)

>>> article.text
'Washington (CNN) -- Not everyone subscribes to a New Year's resolution...'

>>> article.top_image
'http://someCDN.com/blah/blah/blah/file.png'

>>> article.movies
['http://youtube.com/path/to/link.com', ...]
```

```pycon
>>> article.nlp()

>>> article.keywords
['New Years', 'resolution', ...]

>>> article.summary
'The study shows that 93% of people ...'
```

```pycon
>>> import newspaper

>>> cnn_paper = newspaper.build('http://cnn.com')

>>> for article in cnn_paper.articles:
>>>     print(article.url)
http://www.cnn.com/2013/11/27/justice/tucson-arizona-captive-girls/
http://www.cnn.com/2013/12/11/us/texas-teen-dwi-wreck/index.html
...

>>> for category in cnn_paper.category_urls():
>>>     print(category)

http://lifestyle.cnn.com
http://cnn.com/world
http://tech.cnn.com
...

>>> cnn_article = cnn_paper.articles[0]
>>> cnn_article.download()
>>> cnn_article.parse()
>>> cnn_article.nlp()
...
```

```pycon
>>> from newspaper import fulltext

>>> html = requests.get(...).text
>>> text = fulltext(html)
```

Newspaper can extract and detect languages *seamlessly*.
If no language is specified, Newspaper will attempt to auto detect a language.

```pycon
>>> from newspaper import Article
>>> url = 'http://www.bbc.co.uk/zhongwen/simp/chinese_news/2012/12/121210_hongkong_politics.shtml'

>>> a = Article(url, language='zh') # Chinese

>>> a.download()
>>> a.parse()

>>> print(a.text[:150])
香港行政长官梁振英在各方压力下就其大宅的违章建
筑（僭建）问题到立法会接受质询，并向香港民众道歉。
梁振英在星期二（12月10日）的答问大会开始之际
在其演说中道歉，但强调他在违章建筑问题上没有隐瞒的
意图和动机。 一些亲北京阵营议员欢迎梁振英道歉，
且认为应能获得香港民众接受，但这些议员也质问梁振英有

>>> print(a.title)
港特首梁振英就住宅违建事件道歉
```

If you are certain that an *entire* news source is in one language, **go ahead and use the same api :)**

```pycon
>>> import newspaper
>>> sina_paper = newspaper.build('http://www.sina.com.cn/', language='zh')

>>> for category in sina_paper.category_urls():
>>>     print(category)
http://health.sina.com.cn
http://eladies.sina.com.cn
http://english.sina.com
...

>>> article = sina_paper.articles[0]
>>> article.download()
>>> article.parse()

>>> print(article.text)
新浪武汉汽车综合 随着汽车市场的日趋成熟，
传统的“集全家之力抱得爱车归”的全额购车模式已然过时，
另一种轻松的新兴 车模式――金融购车正逐步成为时下消费者购
买爱车最为时尚的消费理念，他们认为，这种新颖的购车
模式既能在短期内
...

>>> print(article.title)
两年双免0手续0利率 科鲁兹掀背金融轻松购_武汉车市_武汉汽
车网_新浪汽车_新浪网
```

## Documentation

Check out [The Documentation] for full and detailed guides using newspaper.

Interested in adding a new language for us? Refer to: [Docs - Adding new languages](https://newspaper.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user_guide/advanced.html#adding-new-languages)

## Features

- Multi-threaded article download framework
- News url identification
- Text extraction from html
- Top image extraction from html
- All image extraction from html
- Keyword extraction from text
- Summary extraction from text
- Author extraction from text
- Google trending terms extraction
- Works in 10+ languages (English, Chinese, German, Arabic, ...)

```pycon
>>> import newspaper
>>> newspaper.languages()

Your available languages are:
input code      full name

  ar              Arabic
  ru              Russian
  nl              Dutch
  de              German
  en              English
  es              Spanish
  fr              French
  he              Hebrew
  it              Italian
  ko              Korean
  no              Norwegian
  fa              Persian
  pl              Polish
  pt              Portuguese
  sv              Swedish
  hu              Hungarian
  fi              Finnish
  da              Danish
  zh              Chinese
  id              Indonesian
  vi              Vietnamese
  sw              Swahili
  tr              Turkish
  el              Greek
  uk              Ukrainian
  bg              Bulgarian
  hr              Croatian
  ro              Romanian
  sl              Slovenian
  sr              Serbian
  et              Estonian
  ja              Japanese
  be              Belarusian
```

## Get it now

Run ✅ `pip3 install newspaper3k` ✅

NOT ⛔ `pip3 install newspaper` ⛔

On python3 you must install `newspaper3k`, **not** `newspaper`. `newspaper` is our python2 library.
Although installing newspaper is simple with [pip](http://www.pip-installer.org/), you will
run into fixable issues if you are trying to install on ubuntu.

**If you are on Debian / Ubuntu**, install using the following:

- Install `pip3` command needed to install `newspaper3k` package:

  ```
  $ sudo apt-get install python3-pip
  ```

- Python development version, needed for Python.h:

  ```
  $ sudo apt-get install python-dev
  ```

- lxml requirements:

  ```
  $ sudo apt-get install libxml2-dev libxslt-dev
  ```

- For PIL to recognize .jpg images:

  ```
  $ sudo apt-get install libjpeg-dev zlib1g-dev libpng12-dev
  ```

NOTE: If you find problem installing `libpng12-dev`, try installing `libpng-dev`.

- Download NLP related corpora:

  ```
  $ curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/codelucas/newspaper/master/download_corpora.py | python3
  ```

- Install the distribution via pip:

  ```
  $ pip3 install newspaper3k
  ```

**If you are on OSX**, install using the following, you may use both homebrew or macports:

```
$ brew install libxml2 libxslt

$ brew install libtiff libjpeg webp little-cms2

$ pip3 install newspaper3k

$ curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/codelucas/newspaper/master/download_corpora.py | python3
```

**Otherwise**, install with the following:

NOTE: You will still most likely need to install the following libraries via your package manager

- PIL: `libjpeg-dev` `zlib1g-dev` `libpng12-dev`
- lxml: `libxml2-dev` `libxslt-dev`
- Python Development version: `python-dev`

```
$ pip3 install newspaper3k

$ curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/codelucas/newspaper/master/download_corpora.py | python3
```

## Development

If you'd like to contribute and hack on the newspaper project, feel free to clone
a development version of this repository locally:

```
git clone git://github.com/codelucas/newspaper.git
```

Once you have a copy of the source, you can embed it in your Python package,
or install it into your site-packages easily:

```
$ pip3 install -r requirements.txt
$ python3 setup.py install
```

Feel free to give our testing suite a shot, everything is mocked!:

```
$ python3 tests/unit_tests.py
```

Planning on tweaking our full-text algorithm? Add the `fulltext` parameter:

```
$ python3 tests/unit_tests.py fulltext
```

## Demo

View a working online demo here: <http://newspaper-demo.herokuapp.com>
This is another working online demo: <http://newspaper.chinazt.cc/>

## LICENSE

Authored and maintained by [Lucas Ou-Yang].

[Parse.ly] sponsored some work on newspaper, specifically focused on
automatic extraction.

Newspaper uses a lot of [python-goose's] parsing code. View their license [here].

Please feel free to [email & contact me] if you run into issues or just would like
to talk about the future of this library and news extraction in general!

modules.name: newspaper.configuration
documentation:
This class holds configuration objects, which can be thought of
as settings.py but dynamic and changing for whatever parent object
holds them. For example, pass in a config object to an Article
object, Source object, or even network methods, and it just works.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

Modify any of these Article / Source properties

Language setting must be set in this method b/c non-occidental
(western) languages require a separate stopwords class.

documentation:
Parses html for a tag like:
<meta http-equiv="refresh" content="0;URL='http://sfbay.craigslist.org/eby/cto/5617800926.html'" />
Example can be found at: https://www.google.com/url?rct=j&sa=t&url=http://sfbay.craigslist.org/eby/cto/
5617800926.html&ct=ga&cd=CAAYATIaYTc4ZTgzYjAwOTAwY2M4Yjpjb206ZW46VVM&usg=AFQjCNF7zAl6JPuEsV4PbEzBomJTUpX4Lg

functions.name: newspaper.utils.to_valid_filename
documentation:
Converts arbitrary string (for us domain name)
into a valid file name for caching

functions.name: newspaper.utils.cache_disk
documentation: Caching extracting category locations & rss feeds for 5 days

functions.name: newspaper.utils.print_duration
documentation: Prints out the runtime duration of a method in seconds

functions.name: newspaper.utils.chunks
documentation: Yield n successive chunks from l

functions.name: newspaper.utils.purge
documentation: Delete files in a dir matching pattern

functions.name: newspaper.utils.clear_memo_cache
documentation: Clears the memoization cache for this specific news domain

functions.name: newspaper.utils.memoize_articles
documentation:
When we parse the <a> links in an <html> page, on the 2nd run
and later, check the <a> links of previous runs. If they match,
it means the link must not be an article, because article urls
change as time passes. This method also uniquifies articles.

functions.name: newspaper.utils.get_useragent
documentation: Uses generator to return next useragent in saved file

functions.name: newspaper.utils.get_available_languages
documentation: Returns a list of available languages and their 2 char input codes

functions.name: newspaper.utils.print_available_languages
documentation: Prints available languages with their full names

functions.name: newspaper.utils.extend_config
documentation:
We are handling config value setting like this for a cleaner api.
Users just need to pass in a named param to this source and we can
dynamically generate a config object for it.

documentation:
Holds the code for cleaning out unwanted tags from the lxml

-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

documentation:
Set appropriate tag names and regexes of tags to remove

methods.name: newspaper.cleaners.DocumentCleaner.clean

methods.name: newspaper.cleaners.DocumentCleaner.clean_body_classes

Removes the `class` attribute from the <body> tag because

modules.name: newspaper.mthreading
documentation:
Anything that has to do with threading in this library
must be abstracted in this file. If we decide to do gevent
also, it will deserve its own gevent file.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: newspaper.mthreading.Worker
documentation: Thread executing tasks from a given tasks queue.

methods.name: newspaper.mthreading.NewsPool.__init__
documentation:
Abstraction of a threadpool. A newspool can accept any number of
source OR article objects together in a list. It allocates one
thread to every source and then joins.

We allocate one thread per source to avoid rate limiting.
5 sources = 5 threads, one per source.

```pycon
>>> import newspaper
>>> from newspaper import news_pool
```

```pycon
>>> cnn_paper = newspaper.build('http://cnn.com')
>>> tc_paper = newspaper.build('http://techcrunch.com')
>>> espn_paper = newspaper.build('http://espn.com')
```

```pycon
>>> papers = [cnn_paper, tc_paper, espn_paper]
>>> news_pool.set(papers)
>>> news_pool.join()
```

\# All of your papers should have their articles html all populated now.
\>>> cnn_paper.articles[50].html
u'\<html>blahblah ... '

methods.name: newspaper.mthreading.NewsPool.join
documentation:
Runs the mtheading and returns when all threads have joined
resets the task.

methods.name: newspaper.mthreading.NewsPool.set
documentation:
news_list can be a list of `Article`, `Source`, or both.

If caller wants to decide how many threads to use, they can use
`override_threads` which takes precedence over all. Otherwise,
this api infers that if the input is all `Source` objects, to
allocate one thread per `Source` to not spam the host.

If both of the above conditions are not true, default to 1 thread.

methods.name: newspaper.parsers.Parser.previousSiblings

documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: newspaper.article.Article

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.__init__
documentation:
The **kwargs argument may be filled with config values, which
is added into the config object

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.build
documentation:
Build a lone article from a URL independent of the source (newspaper).
Don't normally call this method b/c it's good to multithread articles
on a source (newspaper) level.

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.download
documentation:
Downloads the link's HTML content, don't use if you are batch async
downloading articles

recursion_counter (currently 1) stops refreshes that are potentially
infinite

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.is_valid_url
documentation:
Performs a check on the url of this link to determine if article
is a real news article or not

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.is_valid_body
documentation:
If the article's body text is long enough to meet
standard article requirements, keep the article

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.is_media_news
documentation:
If the article is related heavily to media:
gallery, video, big pictures, etc

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.nlp
documentation: Keyword extraction wrapper

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.get_parse_candidate
documentation:
A parse candidate is a wrapper object holding a link hash of this
article and a final_url of the article

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.build_resource_path
documentation: Must be called after computing HTML/final URL

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.get_resource_path
documentation:
Every article object has a special directory to store data in from
initialization to garbage collection

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.set_reddit_top_img
documentation:
Wrapper for setting images. Queries known image attributes
first, then uses Reddit's image algorithm as a fallback.

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.set_html
documentation: Encode HTML before setting it

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.set_article_html

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.set_top_img_no_check
documentation:
Provide 2 APIs for images. One at "top_img", "imgs"
and one at "top_image", "images"

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.set_imgs
documentation:
The motive for this method is the same as above, provide APIs
for both `article.imgs` and `article.images`

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.set_keywords
documentation: Keys are stored in list format

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.set_authors
documentation: Authors are in ["firstName lastName", "firstName lastName"] format

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.set_summary
documentation:
Summary here refers to a paragraph of text from the
title text and body text

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.set_meta_language
documentation: Save langauges in their ISO 2-character form

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.set_meta_keywords

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.set_movies

Parse ArticleDownloadState -> log readable status
-> maybe throw ArticleException

methods.name: newspaper.article.Article.throw_if_not_parsed_verbose
documentation:

-> maybe throw ArticleException
Article objects abstract an online news article page

modules.name: newspaper.outputformatters
documentation: Output formatting to text via lxml xpath nodes abstracted in this file.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

methods.name: newspaper.outputformatters.OutputFormatter.update_language
documentation:
Required to be called before the extraction process in some
cases because the stopwords_class has to set incase the lang
is not latin based

methods.name: newspaper.outputformatters.OutputFormatter.get_formatted
documentation:
Returns the body text of an article, and also the body article
html if specified. Returns in (text, html) form

methods.name: newspaper.outputformatters.OutputFormatter.links_to_text
documentation:
Cleans up and converts any nodes that should be considered
text into text.

methods.name: newspaper.outputformatters.OutputFormatter.remove_negativescores_nodes
documentation:
If there are elements inside our top node that have a
negative gravity score, let's give em the boot.

methods.name: newspaper.outputformatters.OutputFormatter.replace_with_text
documentation:
Replace common tags with just text so we don't have any crazy formatting issues so replace
, , , etc.... With whatever text is inside them. code : http://lxml.de/api/lxml.etree-module.html#strip_tags

methods.name: newspaper.outputformatters.OutputFormatter.remove_empty_tags
documentation:
It's common in top_node to exit tags that are filled with data
within properties but not within the tags themselves, delete them

methods.name: newspaper.outputformatters.OutputFormatter.remove_trailing_media_div
documentation:
Punish the *last top level* node in the top_node if it's
DOM depth is too deep. Many media non-content links are
eliminated: "related", "loading gallery", etc

modules.name: newspaper.nlp
documentation: Anything natural language related should be abstracted into this file.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: newspaper.nlp.load_stopwords
documentation: Loads language-specific stopwords for keyword selection

functions.name: newspaper.nlp.score
documentation: Score sentences based on different features

functions.name: newspaper.nlp.split_words
documentation: Split a string into array of words

functions.name: newspaper.nlp.keywords
documentation:
Get the top 10 keywords and their frequency scores ignores blacklisted
words in stopwords, counts the number of occurrences of each word, and
sorts them in reverse natural order (so descending) by number of
occurrences.

functions.name: newspaper.nlp.split_sentences
documentation: Split a large string into sentences

functions.name: newspaper.nlp.sentence_position
documentation:
Different sentence positions indicate different
probability of being an important sentence.

methods.name: newspaper.videos.extractors.VideoExtractor.get_video_tag

Extracts a list of video from Article top node

modules.name: newspaper.settings
documentation:
Unlike configuration.py, this file is meant for static, entire project
encompassing settings, like memoization and caching file directories.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

modules.name: newspaper.network
documentation:
All code involving requests and responses over the http network
must be abstracted in this file.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: newspaper.network.get_request_kwargs
documentation:
This Wrapper method exists b/c some values in req_kwargs dict
are methods which need to be called every time we make a request

functions.name: newspaper.network.get_html
documentation: HTTP response code agnostic

functions.name: newspaper.network.get_html_2XX_only
documentation:
Consolidated logic for http requests from newspaper. We handle error cases:
- Attempt to find encoding of the html by using HTTP header. Fallback to
  'ISO-8859-1' if not provided.
- Error out if a non 2XX HTTP response code is returned.

functions.name: newspaper.network.multithread_request
documentation:
Request multiple urls via mthreading, order of urls & requests is stable
returns same requests but with response variables filled.

classes.name: newspaper.network.MRequest
documentation:
Wrapper for request object for multithreading. If the domain we are
crawling is under heavy load, the self.resp will be left as None.
If this is the case, we still want to report the url which has failed
so (perhaps) we can try again later.

modules.name: newspaper.urls
documentation:
Newspaper treats urls for news articles as critical components.
Hence, we have an entire module dedicated to them.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: newspaper.urls.remove_args
documentation: Remove all param arguments from a url.

functions.name: newspaper.urls.redirect_back
documentation:
Some sites like Pinterest have api's that cause news
args to direct to their site with the real news url as a
GET param. This method catches that and returns our param.

functions.name: newspaper.urls.prepare_url
documentation:
Operations that purify a url, removes arguments,
redirects, and merges relatives with absolutes.

functions.name: newspaper.urls.valid_url
documentation:
Is this URL a valid news-article url?

Perform a regex check on an absolute url.

First, perform a few basic checks like making sure the format of the url
is right, (scheme, domain, tld).

Second, make sure that the url isn't some static resource, check the
file type.

Then, search of a YYYY/MM/DD pattern in the url. News sites
love to use this pattern, this is a very safe bet.

Separators can be [\.-/_]. Years can be 2 or 4 digits, must
have proper digits 1900-2099. Months and days can be
ambiguous 2 digit numbers, one is even optional, some sites are
liberal with their formatting also matches snippets of GET
queries with keywords inside them. ex: asdf.php?topic_id=blahlbah
We permit alphanumeric, _ and -.

Our next check makes sure that a keyword is within one of the
separators in a url (subdomain or early path separator).
cnn.com/story/blah-blah-blah would pass due to "story".

We filter out articles in this stage by aggressively checking to
see if any resemblance of the source& domain's name or tld is
present within the article title. If it is, that's bad. It must
be a company link, like 'cnn is hiring new interns'.

We also filter out articles with a subdomain or first degree path
on a registered bad keyword.

functions.name: newspaper.urls.url_to_filetype
documentation:
Input a URL and output the filetype of the file
specified by the url. Returns None for no filetype.
'http://blahblah/images/car.jpg' -> 'jpg'
'http://yahoo.com'               -> None

returns a url's domain, this method exists to
encapsulate all url code into this file

functions.name: newspaper.urls.is_abs_url
documentation: this regex was brought to you by django!

modules.name: newspaper.source
documentation:
Source objects abstract online news source websites & domains.
www.cnn.com would be its own source.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

documentation:
The config object for this source will be passed into all of this
source's children articles unless specified otherwise or re-set.

Encapsulates download and basic parsing with lxml. May be a
good idea to split this into download() and parse() methods.

Delete rejected articles, if there is an articles param,
purge from there, otherwise purge from source instance.

Reference this StackOverflow post for some of the wonky
syntax below:
http://stackoverflow.com/questions/1207406/remove-items-from-a-
list-while-iterating-in-python

methods.name: newspaper.source.Source._get_category_urls

The domain param is **necessary**, see .utils.cache_disk for reasons.
the boilerplate method is so we can use this decorator right.
We are caching categories for 1 day.

Sources are abstractions of online news vendors like huffpost or cnn.
domain     =  'www.cnn.com'
scheme     =  'http'
categories =  ['http://cnn.com/world', 'http://money.cnn.com']
feeds      =  ['http://cnn.com/rss.atom', ..]
articles   =  [<article obj>, <article obj>, ..]
brand      =  'cnn'

modules.name: newspaper.images

The following image extraction implementation was taken from an old
copy of Reddit's source code.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

documentation: Calculate the entropy of an image

If the image is taller than it is wide, square it off. determine
which pieces to cut off based on the entropy pieces

modules.name: newspaper.extractors
documentation:
Newspaper uses much of python-goose's extraction code. View their license:
https://github.com/codelucas/newspaper/blob/master/GOOSE-LICENSE.txt

Keep all html page extraction code within this file. Abstract any
lxml or soup parsing code in the parsers.py file!
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.update_language
documentation:
Required to be called before the extraction process in some
cases because the stopwords_class has to set incase the lang
is not latin based

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_authors
documentation:
Fetch the authors of the article, return as a list
Only works for english articles

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_publishing_date
documentation:
3 strategies for publishing date extraction. The strategies
are descending in accuracy and the next strategy is only
attempted if a preferred one fails.

1. Pubdate from URL
2. Pubdate from metadata
3. Raw regex searches in the HTML + added heuristics

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_title
documentation:
Fetch the article title and analyze it

Assumptions:
- title tag is the most reliable (inherited from Goose)
- h1, if properly detected, is the best (visible to users)
- og:title and h1 can help improve the title extraction
- python == is too strict, often we need to compare filtered
  versions, i.e. lowercase and ignoring special chars

Explicit rules:
1. title == h1, no need to split
2. h1 similar to og:title, use h1
3. title contains h1, title contains og:title, len(h1) > len(og:title), use h1
4. title starts with og:title, use og:title
5. use title, after splitting

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.split_title
documentation: Split the title to best part possible

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_feed_urls
documentation:
Takes a source url and a list of category objects and returns
a list of feed urls

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_favicon
documentation:
Extract the favicon from a website http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Favicon
<link rel="shortcut icon" type="image/png" href="favicon.png" />
<link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="favicon.png" />

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_meta_lang
documentation: Extract content language from meta

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_meta_content
documentation:
Extract a given meta content form document.
Example metaNames:
    "meta[name=description]"
    "meta[name=keywords]"
    "meta[property=og:type]"

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_meta_img_url
documentation: Returns the 'top img' as specified by the website

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_meta_type
documentation: Returns meta type of article, open graph protocol

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_meta_description
documentation: If the article has meta description set in the source, use that

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_meta_keywords
documentation: If the article has meta keywords set in the source, use that

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_canonical_link
documentation:
Return the article's canonical URL

Gets the first available value of:
1. The rel=canonical tag
2. The og:url tag

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_img_urls
documentation: Return all of the images on an html page, lxml root

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_first_img_url
documentation:
Retrieves the first image in the 'top_node'
The top node is essentially the HTML markdown where the main
article lies and the first image in that area is probably signifigcant.

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor._get_urls
documentation:
Return a list of urls or a list of (url, title_text) tuples
if specified.

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_urls
documentation:
`doc_or_html`s html page or doc and returns list of urls, the regex
flag indicates we don't parse via lxml and just search the html.

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_category_urls
documentation:
Inputs source lxml root and source url, extracts domain and
finds all of the top level urls, we are assuming that these are
the category urls.
cnn.com --> [cnn.com/latest, world.cnn.com, cnn.com/asia]

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.is_boostable
documentation:
A lot of times the first paragraph might be the caption under an image
so we'll want to make sure if we're going to boost a parent node that
it should be connected to other paragraphs, at least for the first n
paragraphs so we'll want to make sure that the next sibling is a
paragraph and has at least some substantial weight to it.

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_siblings_content
documentation: Adds any siblings that may have a decent score to this node

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_siblings_score
documentation:
We could have long articles that have tons of paragraphs
so if we tried to calculate the base score against
the total text score of those paragraphs it would be unfair.
So we need to normalize the score based on the average scoring
of the paragraphs within the top node.
For example if our total score of 10 paragraphs was 1000
but each had an average value of 100 then 100 should be our base.

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.update_score
documentation:
Adds a score to the gravityScore Attribute we put on divs
we'll get the current score then add the score we're passing
in to the current.

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.update_node_count
documentation: Stores how many decent nodes are under a parent node

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.is_highlink_density
documentation:
Checks the density of links within a node, if there is a high
link to text ratio, then the text is less likely to be relevant

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.get_score
documentation: Returns the gravityScore as an integer from this node

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.nodes_to_check
documentation:
Returns a list of nodes we want to search
on like paragraphs and tables

methods.name: newspaper.extractors.ContentExtractor.post_cleanup
documentation:
Remove any divs that looks like non-content, clusters of links,
or paras with no gusto; add adjacent nodes which look contenty
